1	import type { PageLoad } from './$types'
2	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client'
3	import { gql } from 'graphql-request'
4	
5	const DOC_GRAPH_QUERY = gql`
6		query DocGraph {
7			docGraph {
8				rootDir
9				documents {
10					id
11					path
12					title
13					dependsOn
14					dependents
15				}
16				pathToId
17				warnings
18				issues {
19					issueId
20					kind
21					path
22					id
23					message
24					relatedPath
25				}
26			}
27		}
28	`
29	
30	const DOC_STALE_QUERY = gql`
31		query DocStale {
32			docStale {
33				id
34			}
35		}
36	`
37	
38	export interface GraphDocument {
39		id: string
40		path: string
41		title: string
42		dependsOn: string[]
43		dependents: string[]
44		staleness: string
45		mediaType: string
46	}
47	
48	export interface GraphIssue {
49		issueId: string
50		kind: string
51		path: string | null
52		id: string | null
53		message: string
54		relatedPath: string | null
55	}
56	
57	interface RawGraphDocument {
58		id: string
59		path: string
60		title: string
61		dependsOn: string[]
62		dependents: string[]
63		staleness?: string
64	}
65	
66	interface DocGraph {
67		rootDir: string
68		documents: RawGraphDocument[]
69		pathToId: string
70		warnings: string[]
71		issues: GraphIssue[]
72	}
73	
74	interface DocGraphResult {
75		docGraph: DocGraph
76	}
77	
78	interface StaleResult {
79		docStale: Array<{ id: string }>
80	}
81	
82	function mediaTypeFromPath(path: string): string {
83		const ext = path.split('.').pop()?.toLowerCase() ?? ''
84		if (ext === 'md') return 'text/markdown'
85		if (['png', 'jpg', 'jpeg', 'gif', 'webp'].includes(ext)) return 'image/*'
86		if (ext === 'svg') return 'image/svg+xml'
87		return 'unknown'
88	}
89	
90	export const load: PageLoad = async ({ fetch, url }) => {
91		const client = createClient(fetch as unknown as typeof globalThis.fetch)
92	
93		const [graphData, staleData] = await Promise.all([
94			client.request<DocGraphResult>(DOC_GRAPH_QUERY),
95			client
96				.request<StaleResult>(DOC_STALE_QUERY)
97				.catch(() => ({ docStale: [] as Array<{ id: string }> }))
98		])
99	
100		const graph = graphData.docGraph
101		const staleIds = new Set(staleData.docStale.map((s) => s.id))
102	
103		let pathToDocId: Record<string, string> = {}
104		try {
105			pathToDocId = JSON.parse(graph.pathToId ?? '{}')
106		} catch {
107			pathToDocId = {}
108		}
109	
110		const documents: GraphDocument[] = graph.documents.map((doc) => ({
111			...doc,
112			staleness: doc.staleness ?? (staleIds.has(doc.id) ? 'stale' : 'fresh'),
113			mediaType: mediaTypeFromPath(doc.path)
114		}))
115	
116		// Parse initial viewport from URL params
117		const zoomParam = parseFloat(url.searchParams.get('zoom') ?? '')
118		const panParam = url.searchParams.get('pan')
119		const panParts = panParam ? panParam.split(',').map(parseFloat) : null
120		const initialTransform =
121			!isNaN(zoomParam) && panParts && panParts.length === 2 && panParts.every((v) => !isNaN(v))
122				? { x: panParts[0], y: panParts[1], k: zoomParam }
123				: null
124	
125		const highlightNodeId = url.searchParams.get('highlight') ?? undefined
126	
127		return {
128			graph: {
129				...graph,
130				documents,
131				pathToDocId,
132				issues: graph.issues ?? []
133			},
134			initialTransform,
135			highlightNodeId
136		}
137	}
138

# Story 1 Final — Document graph: visible edges

> **Codex review unavailable.** Codex harness was dispatched twice and hung at 0
> bytes for >5min each time. Final plan below merges the original plan with a
> fresh-eyes audit performed directly against the codebase.

## Diagnosis

Original plan diagnosis confirmed by direct file inspection:

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte:107` —
  arrow marker uses `fill-border-line dark:fill-dark-border-line`
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte:165` —
  edge `<line>` uses `stroke-border-line dark:stroke-dark-border-line`
- `tailwind.config.js` confirms `border-line: #E4DDD0` vs `bg-canvas: #F4EFE6`
  (~1.05:1 contrast) and `dark-border-line: #34302A` vs `dark-bg-canvas: #1A1815`
  (~1.4:1) — far below WCAG AA 3:1 for non-text graphics.
- Stroke width 1.5, opacity 0.6 compound the issue.
- Labels at line 187 already use `fill-fg-muted dark:fill-dark-fg-muted` —
  proposed token is proven to compile through the same generator.

Audit additions:

- **Sole consumer**: `D3Graph.svelte` is imported in exactly one place:
  `routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/+page.svelte:6`. No bead
  graph, run graph, storybook usage, or other consumers. Class swap is fully
  scoped.
- **No existing graph e2e**: no `graph.e2e.ts` exists; sibling pattern
  (`drain.e2e.ts`, `personas.e2e.ts`) is established and `playwright.config.ts`
  is already wired.

## Final bead list

1. **bead-A** — `fix(web): doc graph edges and arrowheads use foreground token, not border-line`
   - Surface: `D3Graph.svelte` lines 107, 165 only.
   - Change: swap `border-line` -> `fg-muted` on edge stroke and arrow marker
     fill; bump `stroke-width` 1.5 -> 1.75 and `stroke-opacity` 0.6 -> 0.9;
     add `stroke-linecap="round"`.
   - ACs: AC1, AC2, AC3, AC4, AC6, AC7.
   - Tier: **cheap** (deterministic ~6-line edit).
   - Deps: none.

2. **bead-B** — `test(web): e2e regression check for doc graph edge color tokens`
   - Surface: new
     `routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts`.
   - Assertions: `<line>` count >= 1; computed `stroke` resolves to
     `rgb(75, 85, 99)` (light `fg-muted` #4B5563); marker `path` fill matches;
     repeat after toggling `dark` class on `<html>` to verify `#B8AF9C`.
   - Tier: **standard** (Playwright theme toggle + GraphQL fixture wiring).
   - Deps: bead-A.

3. **bead-C** — `docs(spec): FEAT-008 AC for graph edge contrast (WCAG AA non-text)`
   - Surface: `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md` only.
   - Tier: **cheap**.
   - Deps: none. Can land before/parallel/after bead-A.

## Tier suggestions

- bead-A: cheap (qwen-class) — pure class swap, easy review.
- bead-B: standard (sonnet-class) — needs theme toggle pattern + selectors;
  cheap models tend to flake on async Playwright + GraphQL fixture work.
- bead-C: cheap — single-line spec edit.

Cost-tiered recommendation: bead-A and bead-C in parallel on cheap tier;
bead-B on standard tier after bead-A merges.

## Flagged ambiguities

1. **`fg-muted` shared with body-secondary text.** Fine for now; if designers
   want a dedicated `graph-edge` token later, bead-A's surface is small enough
   to revisit. Flag, do not block.
2. **Opacity 0.9 on dense graphs** may look noisy. Mitigation already in plan:
   keep stroke-width at 1.5 if 1.75 reads heavy on the densest fixture.
3. **bead-B fixture**: needs a project with >=1 dependency edge in the e2e
   GraphQL mock. If no fixture currently produces edges, bead-B includes the
   fixture work; otherwise reuse existing one. Verify in implementation.
4. **Theme-toggle plumbing in tests**: codebase pattern for toggling dark mode
   in Playwright not audited; bead-B may need `addInitScript` to set theme
   pre-load. Minor unknown, tier-appropriate for standard.
5. **No visual regression infra** (Playwright screenshot snapshots) detected.
   Sticking with computed-style assertions per plan is the right call —
   screenshot snapshots would expand scope.

## Path

data.text: The test is failing because Tailwind v4 doesn't generate `fill-*`/`stroke-*` utilities for legacy config color tokens — so bead-A's class swap looks correct in source but produces no CSS. To make the regression test actually exercise the contract (AC5), I'll switch the d3 stroke/fill from those non-existent utilities to `currentColor`, with the parent group carrying the existing `text-fg-muted` class (which Tailwind v4 _does_ generate).

input.new_string:
// Tailwind v4 doesn't generate fill-/stroke- utilities for legacy
			// config color tokens (only text-/bg-/border- variants), so route the
			// edge tint through `currentColor` and let the existing text-fg-muted
			// utility on the host group resolve it for both themes.
			svgSel
				.append('defs')
				.append('marker')
				.attr('id', 'ddx-arrow')
				.attr('viewBox', '0 -5 10 10')
				.attr('refX', 28)
				.attr('refY', 0)
				.attr('markerWidth', 6)
				.attr('markerHeight', 6)
				.attr('orient', 'auto')
				.attr('class', 'text-fg-muted dark:text-dark-fg-muted')
				.append('path')
				.attr('d', 'M0,-5L10,0L0,5')
				.attr('fill', 'currentColor')

renders with stroke rgb(75, 85, 99) (#4B5563 fg-muted) in light theme and

data.text:
Committed. Test passes (AC1–AC5 satisfied):

- **AC1**: `graph.e2e.ts` created at `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/`
- **AC2**: asserts `<line>` count ≥ 1
- **AC3**: light-theme — edge stroke and `defs marker#ddx-arrow path` fill computed style = `rgb(75, 85, 99)`
- **AC4**: after `document.documentElement.classList.add('dark')`, both = `rgb(184, 175, 156)`
- **AC5**: passes via `bun run test:e2e` (1 passed, 14.7s)

A small companion edit to `D3Graph.svelte` was required: Tailwind v4 doesn't generate `fill-*`/`stroke-*` utilities for legacy config color tokens, so bead-A's class swap landed in source but produced no CSS. Routed edge/marker tint through `currentColor` + `text-fg-muted dark:text-dark-fg-muted` on the host group/marker (Tailwind v4 does generate `text-*` variants), preserving both themes.
